data.title: Fiserv to acquire remaining stake in AIB Merchant Services
data.description:
• Global payments and fintech company Fiserv (NYSE:FI) will acquire the remaining 49.9% stake in AIB Merchant Services (AIBMS) from AIB Group, making AIBMS a wholly owned subsidiary.
  • AIBMS, founded in 2007, is one of Ireland’s largest payment solution providers and a leading e-commerce acquirer in Europe.  The acquisition is aimed at driving continued growth for Fiserv (NYSE:FI) in Ireland and the broader European market
  • The transaction is expected to close in Q3.

data.title: France trade deficit widens more than expected in April
data.description:
• France’s trade deficit widened to €8 billion in April 2025 from an upwardly revised €6.3 billion in March, exceeding market expectations of a €6 billion gap.
  • This marks the largest trade shortfall since last September, as exports dropped 5.9% month-over-month to €49.3 billion.
  • Meanwhile, imports declined at a slower pace of 2.4% to €57.2 billion.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWQ)

Currency: (EUR:USD)

data.title: HIVE Digital achieves 58% peak hash rate growth in one month
data.description:
• HIVE Digital Technologies (NASDAQ:HIVE) Friday reported a major operational milestone—surpassing 10 Exahash per second (EH/s) in global Bitcoin mining capacity, up from 6 EH/s at March quarter-end.
  • Additionally, HIVE's peak hashrate increased 58% month-over-month from 6.6 EH/s in April to 10.4 EH/s in May.
  • HIVE operates globally across 9 time zones and 5 languages, using 100% hydroelectric power to deliver sustainable computing at scale for both Bitcoin mining and AI data centers.
  • The company is on pace to reach 11.5 EH/s by the end of June and continues to accelerate toward its 25 EH/s target by Q4 2025, it said in a statement.

data.title: Amazon said to have cut jobs in books division, including Goodreads and Kindle units
data.description:
• Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) has reportedly laid off employees in its books division, including roles at its Goodreads review platform and Kindle units.
  • The Seattle-based retailer said the cuts affected fewer than 100 workers and were part of an effort to boost efficiency and streamline operations, as per media reports.

  • "As part of our ongoing work to make our teams and programs operate more efficiently, and to better align with our business roadmap, we’ve made the difficult decision to eliminate a small number of roles within the Books organization," an Amazon spokesperson said, as per Reuters.

  • Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) has been implementing job cuts in stages, with recent reductions affecting its devices and services division, Wondery podcast unit, and teams in its retail stores and communications departments.

data.title: Chinese authorities caution BYD, rivals to self-regulate as price war intensifies - report
data.description:
The Chinese authorities summoned major electric vehicle manufacturers, including BYD, urging them to self-regulate as the ongoing price war escalates.

The Ministry of Industry and Information Technology, along with the market regulator and top economic planning agency, called for senior executives from leading EV makers—including BYD (OTCPK:BYDDF), Geely (OTCPK:GELYF), and Xiaomi (OTCPK:XIACF)—to Beijing, Bloomberg reported, citing people familiar with the matter.

The authorities urged automakers to self-regulate and avoid selling vehicles below cost or offering excessive discounts. They warned that such practices could lead to unsustainable competition, financial strain across the supply chain, and potential bankruptcies of weaker firms. The government also highlighted issues such as “zero-mileage” cars and mounting debts that are straining cash flows.

The officials expressed concerns about the long-running price war, which has intensified following BYD’s (OTCPK:BYDDF) recent price cuts of up to 34%.

The China Automobile Manufacturers Association, without mentioning BYD (OTCPK:BYDDF) , said that action taken by a particular company had sparked a fresh round of "price war panic," sending the industry into a "vicious cycle" and endangering the supply chain. “Disorderly price wars intensify vicious competition, further compressing corporate profit margins,” the association commented.

Llist of Chinese electric vehicle manufacturers includes: BYD Seal (OTCPK:BYDDF) (OTCPK:BYDDY), Nio (NYSE:NIO), Li Auto (NASDAQ:LI), XPeng (NYSE:XPEV), Xiaomi's (OTCPK:XIACF) (OTCPK:XIACY), Geely (OTCPK:GELYF) (OTCPK:GELYY)

data.title: Right Season appoints Kristian Thorlund as CEO and Director
Harvard secures temporary relief from Trump’s ban on foreign students
data.description:
• Harvard University secured a temporary reprieve from President Donald Trump’s ban on international students entering the U.S.
  • On Thursday, U.S. District Judge Allison Burroughs ruled that the government cannot enforce Trump’s proclamation, which deepened his ongoing dispute with Harvard over foreign student policies.
  •  The decision came after Harvard amended a May 23 lawsuit challenging a separate federal order aimed at blocking the university from enrolling international students—a move Burroughs had previously halted.
  • The Trump administration has mounted a multifaceted offensive against the nation’s oldest university, suspending billions in grants and other funding while also proposing to revoke its tax-exempt status—moves that have triggered a wave of legal challenges.
     

  • Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

  • More on Harvard University

  • Trump admin hits Harvard University with $450M more in cuts
  • Trump administration blocks future federal grants to Harvard
  • Harvard sues Trump administration over funding freeze
  • Trump: Harvard's tax-exempt status will be rescinded

data.title: ServiceTitan signals $910M–$920M 2026 revenue outlook with 27% Q1 growth while advancing AI and commercial initiatives
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: ServiceTitan (TTAN) Q1 2026

Management View

  • CEO Ara Mahdessian reported that ServiceTitan delivered 29% subscription revenue growth, 27% total revenue growth, and record operating margins, which improved by 560 basis points year-over-year. Mahdessian highlighted, "Our sales team delivered consistent performance. Our product and engineering teams are executing well on our road map and I'm pleased that several of the largest and most strategic accounts, both residential and commercial went live this quarter." He emphasized the expansion into new trades, particularly through customer-led adoption and cited customer success stories with A1 Garage Door and Guild Garage as evidence of the platform's ROI and scalability.

  • President Vahe Kuzoyan stated that enterprise customers are "increasingly the tip of the spear for our growth" and noted strong demand for standardizing operations around AI and automation. Kuzoyan said, "Our new field assist technology, empowering technicians to ask Titan Intelligence questions from the field went live during Q1. And in April, we launched our new Contact Center Pro virtual agents, native to our platform, unlocking opportunities to automate our customers' CS operations."

  • CFO Dave Sherry reported, "Q1 gross transaction volume, or GTV, was $17.7 billion, up 22% year-over-year with healthy growth from both residential and commercial customers. Q1 total revenue was $215.7 million, up 27% year-over-year." Sherry added, "Q1 free cash flow was negative $22.3 million better than negative $24.6 million for the prior year first quarter in spite of greater cash bonus attainment this year compared to the prior year."

Outlook

  • Sherry provided guidance for Q2, expecting total revenue in the range of $228 million to $230 million and operating income in the range of $17 million to $18 million. Full year fiscal 2026 guidance is for total revenue in the range of $910 million to $920 million and operating income in the range of $54 million to $59 million. Management highlighted, "This has historically been most positively pronounced during Q2 due to weather-driven seasonality in many trades. This leads to typically stronger usage revenue mix in Q2, which carries more variability than subscription."

Financial Results

  • Q1 total revenue reached $215.7 million, reflecting a 27% year-over-year increase. Subscription revenue grew to $162.7 million, up 29% year-over-year, and usage revenue reached $45.3 million, up 22% year-over-year. Platform gross margin was 79.7%, improving by over 300 basis points year-over-year, while total gross margin was 73.6%, up 390 basis points year-over-year. Q1 operating income was $16.2 million, representing a 7.5% record operating margin. Net dollar retention was greater than 110% for the quarter. Sherry noted that the timing of expenses was more favorable than in prior years and encouraged not to analyze each quarter in isolation due to potential timing variances.

Q&A

  • Kasthuri Gopalan Rangan, Goldman Sachs: Asked about the potential impact of tariffs on the business and whether this could extend equipment lifetimes and service opportunities. Mahdessian responded, "There are compound factors from tariffs, which could affect our customers' growth. It's possible that we see a return of supply chain inflation. Although I would say in the past, our customers have proven their ability to pass through rising costs."

  • Joshua Phillip Baer, Morgan Stanley: Inquired about the stacking S-curve strategy and whether new trades and Pro products will drive durable growth. Kuzoyan replied, "We're big proponents of focus...today, we're focused on enterprise, commercial, Pro and roofing."

  • Michael James Turrin, Wells Fargo: Asked about seasonality and Pro product attach rates. Sherry explained, "GTV is a bigger factor in Q2 than any other quarter in the year...The Pro products are the fastest-growing portion of our business, which is what you're seeing in the subscription growth performance versus prior year."

  • David E. Hynes, Canaccord Genuity: Sought updates on commercial bookings and product status. Mahdessian stated, "Commercial bookings and customer go-lives are performing well...We activated more ARR in 28 hours than our enterprise commercial team typically does in a month."

  • Brent Alan Bracelin, Piper Sandler: Asked about commercial market penetration. Kuzoyan said, "We're still very early days here in terms of our penetration in commercial."

  • Terry Tillman, Truist: Asked about the impact of customer go-lives on subscription and usage revenue. Sherry confirmed, "On the visibility of subscription revenue, it's pretty clear given the ramp in ASC 606 nature of the contracts. That's pretty high visibility when a customer goes live."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analyst sentiment was generally positive, with multiple congratulations on growth and strong execution, but several questions probed deeper into macro risks, commercial traction, and product expansion.

  • Management maintained a confident and upbeat tone in prepared remarks and Q&A, emphasizing focus, customer success, and durability of growth. Mahdessian used phrases like "our opportunity to transform the lives of every, hard-working contractor in the trades" and Sherry provided prudent answers on guidance, indicating careful optimism.

  • Compared to the previous quarter, both analysts and management continued to express optimism, but with ongoing attention to macro risks and the impact of seasonality and tariffs.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Q1 total revenue grew to $215.7 million from $209.3 million in Q4, and subscription revenue increased from $156.7 million to $162.7 million. Gross transaction volume rose from $17 billion to $17.7 billion. Platform gross margin improved to 79.7% from 76.7%. Operating income and margin also increased significantly from the previous quarter. Full year 2026 revenue guidance was raised from a prior range of $895 million–$905 million to $910 million–$920 million. Management’s tone remained confident, with more concrete examples of AI product launches and deeper commercial go-lives in Q1.

  • Analysts continued to focus on commercial expansion, macroeconomic impacts, the effect of tariffs, and the scaling of new products. Management highlighted greater visibility on subscription revenue and recurring themes of AI integration and product-led growth.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management discussed macroeconomic uncertainty, citing potential impacts from tariffs and supply chain inflation. Mahdessian said, "the macro is outside of our control, we elect to forecast pretty prudently, especially on GTV."

  • Seasonality, particularly weather-driven usage volatility, remains a risk factor, with Sherry noting, "Q2 is particularly sensitive to weather, both in terms of absolute temperature but also degree of temperature change because usage is a larger portion of our mix in Q2."

  • Analysts raised questions about the durability of demand in a changing macro environment, the pace of commercial penetration, and the continued ability to upsell Pro products.

Final Takeaway

ServiceTitan starts fiscal 2026 with strong momentum, highlighted by robust revenue growth, margin expansion, and deepening enterprise and commercial customer adoption. Management raises full-year revenue guidance, emphasizes the ongoing rollout of AI-native products, and underscores continued focus on durable growth and operational discipline in the face of macroeconomic uncertainty. The company signals confidence in its ability to deliver long-term value as it scales its platform across more trades and customer segments.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Docusign in charts: Subscription revenue maintains single-digit growth pace in Q1; Billings up 4% Y/Y
Germany's exports drop in April, denting trade surplus
data.description:
• Germany recorded a trade surplus of €14.60B in April, lower than estimates of €20.2B.
  • The figure is lower than the previous month's €21.3B.
  • German exports fell by 1.7%, compared to March 2025, while imports rose by 3.9%.
